data.title: Ringgit Opens Firmer Near Strongest Level Since 2018 After Fed Holds Rates
data.description:
The Malaysian ringgit opened slightly stronger against the US dollar on Thursday, supported by confidence in Malaysia’s economic growth and resilient domestic fundamentals, as investors digested the Federal Reserve’s decision to keep interest rates unchanged.

At 8 am, the local currency was quoted at 3.9130/9230 versus the greenback, up from Wednesday’s close of 3.9175/9235. This puts the ringgit near its strongest level against the US dollar since April 2018, when it touched 3.9030.

Bank Negara Malaysia Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour yesterday reaffirmed Malaysia’s solid economic fundamentals, highlighting the country’s diversified economy and resilient domestic demand.

“No single country accounts for more than 15% of Malaysia’s exports. That shows how diversified we are,” he said.

On fiscal matters, Abdul Rasheed pointed to subsidy rationalisation for fuel, electricity, water, and diesel as reflecting policy credibility.

The Finance Ministry added that the shift from bulk RON95 petrol subsidies to targeted BUDI95 support is expected to generate annual savings of RM2.5 billion to RM4 billion.

Sales tax on low-value goods contributed RM817 million in revenue last year, up from RM476 million in 2024.

The ringgit also strengthened against a basket of major currencies and regional peers.

It climbed versus the euro to 4.6870/6990 and the Japanese yen to 2.5558/5625, but eased against the British pound to 5.4097/4235.

Gains were seen against regional currencies, rising to 3.1019/1103 versus the Singapore dollar, 12.5658/6097 versus the Thai baht, 233.9/234.7 versus the Indonesian rupiah, and 6.66/6.68 versus the Philippine peso.

Bernama

data.title: Profit-Taking Drags Bursa Lower As FBM KLCI Slides 1.46%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended lower for a second straight session as profit-taking gathered pace, dragging most sectors into the red and signalling a broad-based market consolidation.

The FBM KLCI dropped 25.60 points, or 1.46%, to 1,730.89 at the close, retreating from its previous session’s gains as selling pressure weighed on index-linked heavyweights.

Market weakness was reflected across the board, with the FBM EMAS sliding 1.17% to 12,691.50, the FBM Shariah index easing 0.95% to 12,278.23 and the FBM 70 declining 0.55% to 17,467.43. The FTSE Bursa Malaysia 4G index also fell 1.26% to 1,022.06, underscoring cautious investor sentiment.

On the trading front, Capital A Bhd was the most active counter, surging five sen to 63.5 sen on strong volume of 111.21 million stocks. Newly ACE Market-debutant ISF Group Bhd also advanced five sen to 53 sen, while Zetrix AI Bhd slipped two sen to 80 sen. Tanco Holdings Bhd closed unchanged at RM1.34 and Public Bank fell 10 sen to RM4.85 amid profit-taking in banking stocks.

Market participants said the decline reflects a near-term breather after recent advances, with investors remaining selective as they reassess valuations and external cues.

data.title: Decliners Swamp Gainers As Market Breadth Turns Deeply Negative
data.description:
The decliners overwhelmingly outnumbered the gainers on Bursa Malaysia at the close on Jan 29, underscoring broad-based selling pressure across the market.

At 5pm, there were 800 losers compared to just 372 gainers, while 532 counters ended unchanged. Turnover slipped to 3.06 billion shares valued at RM3.95 billion, reflecting active but risk-averse trading.

Consumer and financial heavyweights led losses as Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd fell RM1.08 to RM34.42, while Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd shed 70 sen to RM33.10. Banking stocks also weakened, with Hong Leong Bank Bhd down 52 sen to RM24.32 and Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd sliding 30 sen to RM21.70. Petronas Gas Bhd slipped 34 sen to RM18.26.

On the upside, heavyweight Nestlé (M) Bhd rose 50 sen to RM114, topping the gainers’ list. VSTECS Bhd climbed 21 sen to RM4.24, while Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd added 10 sen to RM2.75. Riverview Rubber Estates Bhd and IOI Properties Group Bhd also posted modest gains as both counters added 10 sen and nine sen, respectively, to close at RM3 and RM3.21.

The sharply negative market breadth pointed to cautious sentiment, with selling pressure outweighing selective bargain hunting despite pockets of resilience among a handful of blue chips.

data.title: Venezuela, Iran May Raise Oil Price Volatility But Supply Unlikely Disrupted
data.description:
MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB Research) has maintained a NEUTRAL call on the oil and gas sector, favouring midstream operators for defensive cash flows.

Dialog Group is highlighted as the top pick with a target price of RM2.17.

MBSB Research said that rising Venezuelan crude production and escalating tensions in the Middle East are increasing short-term oil price volatility but are unlikely to disrupt physical supply.

Venezuela plans to boost crude output by 18% in 2026, translating to an incremental 200 thousand to 300 thousand barrels per day over the next 12 to 24 months.

MBSB Research said this will be achieved through capital-light workovers and optimisation rather than large-scale upstream redevelopment, with structural constraints such as weak economics, degraded infrastructure and political risk limiting the potential impact on global supply.

MBSB Research noted that recent tensions between the United States, Israel and Iran have materially lifted the geopolitical risk premium embedded in oil prices.

However, no disruption has occurred to Iranian exports or shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

The research house reported that markets are pricing headlines rather than actual barrel losses.

Near-term oil prices are likely to experience headline-driven spikes while medium-term fundamentals remain well supplied, pulling prices back toward equilibrium once tensions ease.

MBSB Research maintained its base-case Brent outlook at US$55 to US$60 per barrel.

The house said upside for upstream and downstream players is limited, making midstream operators with take-or-pay contracts and stable cash flows the preferred defensive exposure.

MBSB Research expects increasing demand for crude storage in the near to mid-term to support the resilience of the midstream segment, underpinning their preference for Dialog Group.

data.title: Asia Markets Pause As Tech Earnings Stir Caution
data.description:
Asia’s stock markets eased on Thursday as mixed earnings from the technology sector prompted investors to take a more cautious stance ahead of Apple’s upcoming results.

South Korea’s benchmark slipped 1.2%, despite a 21% gain so far this month, while the tech-heavy Taiwan market edged down 0.6%, having surged almost 14% year-to-date.

Japan’s Nikkei was down 0.1%, pressured by sharp swings in the yen and rising domestic bond yields. MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan fell 0.6%.

The cautious mood came even as Samsung Electronics posted a tripling of its operating profit, benefiting from surging chip prices amid the AI investment boom.

On Wall Street, S&P 500 futures declined 0.2% and Nasdaq futures lost 0.1% after mixed results from major tech firms.

Microsoft shares fell 6.5% in extended trading on concerns that its massive capital expenditure may not yield sufficient returns.

Meanwhile, Meta raised its revenue and capex outlook for 2026, sending shares up 8% after hours and adding roughly US$140 billion to its market value.

“A common theme so far from META and MSFT is the larger-than-expected capex spending, indicating the upward momentum for AI spending,” analysts at JPMorgan noted.

“The difference, they added, was Meta also sharply raised its 2026 revenue outlook to well above market expectations.”

Attention now turns to Apple, with JPMorgan expecting earnings to beat consensus thanks to stronger iPhone 17 demand and slower growth in expenses.

The bank raised its end-2026 price target to US$315, up from US$305.

Currency markets showed continued volatility, with the US dollar under pressure amid policy uncertainty and growing debt concerns.

US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent insisted the administration still favoured a “strong dollar” policy, even as President Trump appeared to condone a weaker currency.

European officials expressed concern over the dollar’s decline, while European Central Bank officials suggested that further euro strength could justify interest rate cuts.

The euro rose 0.2% to US$1.1973, while the dollar slipped 0.4% against the Swiss franc to 0.7657 and 0.3% versus the yen to 152.97.

“Further dollar weakness would increase the likelihood of the ECB and other central banks cutting interest rates, as well as emerging markets repeating last year’s outperformance,” said Jack Allen-Reynolds, an economist at Capital Economics.

Investor appetite for hard assets remained strong, with gold climbing 1.8% to US$5,485 an ounce for a 27% gain this month, silver and copper also advancing.

Oil prices hit a four-month high, with Brent up 0.6% to US$68.80 a barrel and US crude rising 0.7% to US$63.65 per barrel, supported by concerns over potential US military action against Iran.

The U.S. Federal Reserve kept interest rates on hold as expected, with Chair Jerome Powell noting a “clearly improving” economic outlook and broad support within the committee for a pause.

Powell declined to comment on whether he would remain a governor after stepping down as Chair in May, amid Trump’s push for more aggressive rate cuts.

data.title: Neutral On Glove Sector Despite Nipah Virus Outbreak
data.description:
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) has maintained a NEUTRAL stance on the glove sector, keeping all three companies under coverage (Top Glove, Hartalega and Kossan) at HOLD.

Top Glove’s target price is RM0.60, Hartalega RM1.10 and Kossan RM1.13, the analysts said, adding that a structural re-rating of the sector is unlikely despite recent market optimism over the Nipah virus outbreak in India’s West Bengal.

HLIB noted that although Nipah carries a high fatality rate and there are no approved vaccines, the virus lacks the transmissibility to become a global pandemic.

Despite Nipah’s high fatality rate and the absence of approved vaccines, we feel that its low transmissibility means that the probability of recent cases morphing into a pandemic is low and thus is unlikely to re-rate the glove sector,” HLIB said in their report.

The recent outbreak in India saw two confirmed infections reported since December 2025, with 196 contacts traced and all testing negative as of 27 January 2026. The development coincided with gains in the Malaysian glove sector yesterday, with Top Glove up 3.3%, Hartalega 6.2% and Kossan 2.0%, despite the ringgit continuing to strengthen against the US dollar this month.

HLIB maintained its forecasts, noting that recent share price corrections have already factored in near-term and long-term sector challenges, leaving a balanced risk-reward profile for investors.

data.title: Hong Kong Exchange Fund Logs Recond Gains In 2025
data.description:
Investment income of Hong Kong’s Exchange Fund in 2025 came in at a record 331 billion Hong Kong dollars (about 42 billion U.S. dollars) amid the global bull market, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) said on Wednesday.

The gains included 142.2 billion Hong Kong dollars on bonds, 33.9 billion Hong Kong dollars on Hong Kong equities and 74.1 billion Hong Kong dollars on other equities. Annual investment return stood at 8 percent, according to the HKMA.

Total assets of the Exchange Fund stood at around 4.15 trillion Hong Kong dollars by the end of December, up 70.4 billion Hong Kong dollars from the end of 2024.

Eddie Yue, chief executive of the HKMA, said that the unprecedented gains came amid solid or even record upturns of major stock markets worldwide. Benefitting from capital inflows into the Hong Kong stock market, the Hang Seng Index rose by 28 percent in 2025.

Yue acknowledged that the exceptional confluence of multiple favorable factors in the global financial markets in 2025 may not last for a long time. In 2026, factors such as global economic conditions, monetary policies of major central banks, developments in artificial intelligence, and geopolitical conflicts could affect the performance of financial markets, he said

data.title: Gold Prices Surge In Malaysia Amid Dollar Weakness And Geopolitical Concerns
data.description:
Gold prices in Malaysia rose sharply on Wednesday, with spot gold hitting RM666.56 per gram, up RM20.55 or 3.2% from the previous day.

The price per kilo climbed to RM666,558, marking a 6.7% increase, while gold per ounce reached RM20,732, per goldprice.org.

The rally comes as the US dollar plunged to near four-year lows amid persistent geopolitical tensions and ahead of the Federal Reserve’s policy announcement.

According to Kelvin Wong, senior market analyst at OANDA, gold’s surge is closely linked to dollar weakness, following remarks from former US President Donald Trump signalling a broad consensus for a weaker greenback.

Ilya Spivak, head of global macro at Tastylive, said markets are adopting a defensive stance ahead of Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s comments.

Deutsche Bank has projected gold could climb to US$6,000 per ounce in 2026, citing strong investment demand from central banks and investors seeking non-dollar and tangible assets.

RHB Research noted that COMEX gold maintained bullish momentum, with resistance near US$5,200 and potential to move toward US$5,500 upon a breakout.

Analysts recommended retaining long positions with support at US$4,800 and US$4,550, underscoring ongoing positive market sentiment.

Silver and other precious metals also recorded gains, with silver up 6.9% to RM29.18 per kg, platinum rising 1.7% to US$2,686.50 per ounce and palladium increasing 1.3% to US$1,959.43. Analysts attribute the momentum to continued demand for hedging amid currency volatility and inflation concerns.

The recent rally reinforces Malaysia’s growing interest in gold as both an investment and a hedge against currency fluctuations, with prices up more than 13.5% over the past month and over 66% year-on-year.

data.title: Bursa Opens Lower As Fed Holds Rates Steady
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened lower, with the FBMKLCI slipping 12.12 points, or 0.69%, to 1,744.37 as investors digested global monetary developments.

The broader market followed suit, with the FBM70 and FBMEMAS declining 0.08% and 0.48% respectively in early trade.

Market activity saw VELESTO leading the volume chart with 15.7 million shares traded at 30 sen, while TWL and ISF posted modest gains. Among top gainers, PANAMY rose 13 sen to RM7.39 and KSENG added 6 sen to RM5.62, while Nestlé and HEIM were notable decliners.

Investors are responding to the Federal Reserve’s decision to keep interest rates unchanged in the 3.50%-3.75% range on Tuesday, following a 10-2 vote by policymakers.

Fed Chair Jerome Powell said risks to inflation and employment had diminished, signalling a cautious approach to further rate cuts.

The steady rate outlook has prompted a rotation in emerging markets, with local equities taking cues from global sentiment.

The local market’s early session was also weighed down by losses in heavyweight counters. Maybank dropped 16 sen to RM11.64 and IHH slid 12 sen to RM8.90, reflecting profit-taking after recent gains.

On the upside, investors bought selectively into mid-cap and small-cap names, with IG BREIT rising 5 sen to RM2.91 on strong trading volume.

The FBMKLCI remains above the 1,740 support level despite the morning dip, supported by positive earnings reports and foreign inflows.

The market is expected to remain sensitive to global interest rate signals and foreign portfolio flows, particularly as investors assess the impact of the Fed’s pause on emerging Asian assets.

As of 9.14 am, total market volume stood at 120.3 million shares valued at RM171.15 million, with advancers outnumbering decliners 117 to 61.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Jan 29, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on Jan 29, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Slides At Midday As Broad-Based Selling Weighs On Market
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia was lower at midday on Thursday, dragged down by broad-based selling across heavyweight counters, with investors turning cautious amid regional weakness.

The FBM KLCI fell 25.12 points or 1.43% to 1,731.37 at 12.35 pm, after moving between an intraday high of 1,753.31 and a low of 1,719.52.

Market breadth was negative, with losses seen across key indices.

The FBM 70 declined 146.33 points or 0.83% to 17,416.90, while the FBM Emas slid 158.30 points or 1.23% to 12,684.09.

The FBM Shariah Index dropped 134.93 points or 1.09% to 12,261.34 and the F4GBM Index eased 13.51 points or 1.31% to 1,021.54.

Trading activity remained active, led by ZETRIX, which was the most traded counter, slipping 2 sen to 80 sen on a volume of about 50.98 million shares.

Capital A was also actively traded, rising 3.5 sen to 62 sen on 50.03 million shares, while Velesto traded flat at 30.5 sen with 40.08 million shares changing hands.

BPURI added half a sen to 30 sen and NICE gained half a sen to 12 sen, both seeing heavy volumes.

On the gainers’ board, Nestle edged up 50 sen to RM114.00, while VSTECS rose 18 sen to RM4.21.

BNASTRA and SCGBHD each gained 7 sen to RM2.15 and RM2.13 respectively, while KJTS advanced 5.5 sen to 87.5 sen.

Meanwhile, selling pressure was evident among blue chips. Fraser & Neave led the losers, falling 90 sen to RM34.60.

Hong Leong Bank dropped 68 sen to RM24.16, Allianz declined 38 sen to RM22.86, PPB slid 38 sen to RM11.02 and Hong Leong Financial Group eased 36 sen to RM21.64.

Overall sentiment remained cautious as investors pared exposure ahead of the afternoon session, with market participants closely watching regional cues and upcoming economic signals.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Retreats At Midday, FBM KLCI Falls 12 Points
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia’s key indices were lower at midday, with the FBM KLCI slipping 12.41 points, or 0.70%, to 1,758.84 as of 12.35 pm.

The broader market followed suit, with the FBM70 down 122.07 points (-0.69%) and the FBMEMAS shedding 93.15 points (-0.72%).

Trading activity remained concentrated in small-cap counters. Debutant ISF led the volume charts, surging 15.5 sen to 48.5 sen on 230.3 million shares.

Other active counters included Velesto (-1.5 sen to 30 sen), Zetrix (+0.5 sen to 82.5 sen) and Tanco (+1 sen to RM1.34).

On the gainers board, DLADY rose 70 sen to RM33.72, followed by KLK (+24 sen to RM20.06) and Allianz (+22 sen to RM23.22).

Meanwhile, Nestle led the losers, down RM1 to RM114.60, alongside Heimbach (-46 sen), MPI (-46 sen), F&N (-30 sen) and UTDPLT (-28 sen).

The market remained cautious as investors digested mixed corporate earnings and external developments, with selling pressure observed across blue-chip and mid-cap stocks.

data.title: US Stocks Move Sideways, Shruggging Off Low-key Fed Meeting
data.description:
Wall Street stocks finished little changed Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, while the dollar rebounded somewhat from the prior session’s slide.

The Fed, as expected, voted to maintain rates at a range between 3.50 percent and 3.75 percent. Fed Chair Jerome Powell said the status-quo move made sense as the central bank monitors economic data after cutting three straight times due to signs of a weakening labor market.

Major US indices, which were directionless before the 1900 GMT Fed announcement, moved in a choppy fashion after the decision.

The S&P 500 finished nearly flat at 6,978.03 after earlier touching above 7,000 points for the first time.

The market had “almost zero reaction” to the Fed, said Art Hogan of B. Riley Wealth Management

“The statement and the press conference really delivered no news,” Hogan said Wednesday afternoon shortly before earnings announcements from Microsoft and other large companies.

The dollar recovered some of its losses from the prior session, when comments from US President Donald Trump seeming to greet a weaker US currency, sending the dollar to a four and a half year low against the euro.

But the strengthening euro was among the factors that dragged on European stocks.

Also weighing on Europe was a downgrade to the German government’s 2026 growth forecast to one percent from 1.3 percent previously.

“The expected stimulus from economic and fiscal policy measures did not materialize quite as quickly or to the extent that we had assumed,” Economy Minister Katherina Reiche told a Berlin press conference.

The CAC 40 in Paris was dragged down by renewed concerns for the luxury sector after market heavyweight LVMH posted a 13 percent slide in annual profit.

LVMH shares tumbled nearly seven percent in response, while British fashion label Burberry, traded in London, fell 4.7 percent.

Elsewhere, the price of gold struck a new peak as skittishness over the dollar pushes some investors towards the metal as a safe-haven investment.

US retail giant Amazon slid 0.6 percent after announcing that it is cutting 16,000 jobs worldwide as the company tries to streamline amid its major push into AI.

But shares in Dutch tech giant ASML, the global leader in the machines that make semiconductors, jumped after the company announced a strong rise in annual profits and a buoyant outlook, while also saying it would cut hundreds of management jobs.

data.title: SGX Opens Higher Amid Fed Pause Optimism
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange (SGX) opened higher on Thursday, with the Straits Times Index (STI) rising 3.83 points, or 0.08%, to 4,913.17 as at 9.05 am.

Market turnover stood at S$171.15 million on a volume of 120.3 million shares, with advancers outpacing decliners 117 to 61.

Banking and technology stocks led early gains, with DBS up 0.24% to S$59.78, OCBC Bank at S$21.27, and ST Engineering trading at S$9.47. Real estate counters also saw moderate buying interest, with Hongkong Land USD at S$8.47 and Keppel at S$11.04. Shipbuilding stock YZJ Shipbuilding rose to S$3.35.

The market opened cautiously higher following the Federal Reserve’s decision to hold interest rates steady at 3.50%-3.75% on Wednesday, signalling confidence in the US economy amid “diminished risks” to inflation and employment.

The Fed’s pause could support regional equities by maintaining stable global liquidity and keeping borrowing costs predictable.

Derivatives trading also showed moderate activity, with equity index futures volumes at 16,740 contracts.

The FTSE China A50 Index futures were at 14,873, while Nikkei 225 futures traded at 53,325. FX futures indicated slight strength in the U.S. dollar, with USD/SGD futures at 1.2600 and USD/CNH at 6.9228.

Other SGX benchmark indices reflected mixed early moves, with the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index at 1,505.10 and the iEdge S-REIT Index at 1,125.12.

Sustainability-focused and regional technology indices showed steady performance, highlighting investor appetite for both defensive and growth segments.

Market participants are likely to track upcoming US economic data and corporate earnings in Asia for further cues, as investor sentiment remains sensitive to global rate policy and the trajectory of inflation in the coming months.

data.title: Gold Surges Past RM697,000 per Kilogram On Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
Gold prices in Malaysia surged sharply on Thursday, rising 4.37% to RM697.52 per gram, or RM697,522 per kilogram, reflecting a strong wave of safe-haven buying amid geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainty.

Silver also advanced, gaining 3.15% to RM464.79 per kilogram.

Analysts attributed the surge to global concerns over US political instability and monetary policy.

“Gold prices are rising on safe-haven demand because of the strange geopolitical situation and even the political situation in the US, (which is) not looking great. There are concerns around Fed independence. And when that happens, investor trust in the financial system gets shaken up,” said ANZ analyst Soni Kumari, as cited by Reuters.

Marex analyst Edward Meir added, “Growing US debt and uncertainty created by signs that the global trade system is splintering into regional blocs as opposed to a US-centric model are leading investors to pile into gold.”

Gold has climbed past RM500,000 per kilogram for the first time this month and is on track for its ninth consecutive session of record-highs.

RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) noted that COMEX gold gapped up to close at US$5,303.60 per ounce on Wednesday, with bullish momentum likely to test the US$5,500 resistance.

“The commodity has been printing a series of higher highs with higher lows, showing that strong bullish momentum is in play,” RHB Research said, maintaining a long position while setting immediate support at US$4,800.

Locally, the price of gold has jumped RM909 per gram over the past day, while monthly gains have reached RM3,225 per gram, reflecting a 18% rise over 30 days.

Over the past year, Malaysian gold investors have seen gains of 69%, while silver has surged 221% in the same period, highlighting a strong shift toward precious metals amid global uncertainty.

The rally has been supported by firm central bank buying, a weaker US dollar, and heightened investor demand for alternatives amid political volatility and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

data.title: Chinese Yuan Rises Against USD, Trades At 6.97
data.description:
The central parity rate of the Chinese currency renminbi, or the yuan, strengthened 103 pips to 6.9755 against the U.S. dollar Wednesday, according to the China Foreign Exchange Trade System.

In China’s spot foreign exchange market, the yuan is allowed to rise or fall by 2 percent from the central parity rate each trading day.

The central parity rate of the yuan against the U.S. dollar is based on a weighted average of prices offered by market makers before the opening of the interbank market each business day.

data.title: Why Everyone Still Checks On Gold (And Why You Should Too)
data.description:
Even when gold isn’t making headlines, everyone still checks it. Lately, with inflation numbers shaking markets and currencies wobbling, gold has been quietly moving and traders notice. It’s like that unpredictable colleague who sometimes drops a comment that changes everything. You might not see it coming, but you can’t ignore it.

At XM, we understand why that matters. Success isn’t about reacting to every headline or chasing big wins. It’s about staying aware, showing up consistently, and understanding the patterns beneath the noise. That’s exactly how XM Traders Club works: steady engagement, real insights, and rewards that grow with your effort — not just your luck.

The key isn’t reacting to every spike. It’s learning to watch trends, connect the dots, and understand why prices move. That bigger picture is what gives traders a real edge.

Applying This To Your Trades

Instead of jumping on every price move, it helps to pause and recognise the common forces behind them:

  • A record high? Is it being driven by safe-haven demand or a weakening dollar?
  • A dip? Could it be profit-taking or shifting economic expectations?
  • Sudden spikes? Are they short-term reactions to news rather than lasting trends?

Understanding these drivers allows traders to respond with intention, rather than reacting on impulse.

Learn From Real Market Moves

Gold doesn’t move in isolation. Every price shift reflects layers of data, market sentiment, and expectations — and the real insight comes when you see how they connect.

That’s what we’ll explore in our upcoming XM Grand Webinar. We’ll break down what’s really moving gold right now — from economic signals and central bank decisions to how trends interact over time. The goal is simple: help traders make informed decisions based on patterns, not panic.

For those who want to go further, XM also lets you track your trading activity over time — so you can see patterns, watch your decisions play out, and turn consistent effort into real learning.

Progress Over Quick Wins

The most reliable trading strategy isn’t about chasing every swing. It’s about spotting patterns, learning from each trade, and staying consistent. XM Traders Club is built around this mindset: participation over time matters more than one-off results. Every trade adds experience, and every step forward counts.

Gold teaches a crucial lesson: insight matters more than prediction. Smart traders observe, learn, and stay consistent — instead of reacting impulsively. By tracking trades and engagement, every move becomes a building block in your trading journey.

Are You Ready To Take The Next Step?

Markets make more sense when viewed as a whole. Trends reveal what single moves cannot. By following your progress and understanding how patterns form over time, effort turns into meaningful growth. XM helps connect those dots — so every trade contributes to knowledge, clarity, and steady progress, not just short-term results.

Ready to turn insight into action?

Join XM Traders Club today and start tracking your trades, learning from the market, and earning rewards for consistent engagement. Every trade counts — make yours work for you.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum Cautious Ahead Of Macro Signals
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ethereum showed limited gains and consolidation on Jan 28 as the broader cryptocurrency market digested macroeconomic uncertainty and awaited key catalysts.

Bitcoin ticked slightly higher within a narrow range near the upper US$80,000s, reflecting subdued buyer conviction and continued risk-off positioning among traders and institutional players. Ethereum outperformed modestly, posting small gains while trading around the mid-US$2,900s amid selective altcoin interest. Overall, the market cap held near the US$3 trillion mark, signalling stabilisation after recent volatility.

Market Dynamics Driving the Pause

Macro risk sensitivity: Investors are positioning cautiously ahead of major Federal Reserve (Fed) guidance and other economic data, tempering crypto risk exposure and favouring traditional assets. Safe-haven flows into gold and other low-risk instruments have influenced digital asset sentiment.

Cost basis and technical support: On-chain data indicates Ethereum trading within a significant cost-basis cluster, potentially reinforcing short-term support while highlighting thinner layers of holder conviction beneath key levels.

Sentiment rebound signs: Broader market analysis suggests the crypto downturn that marked late 2025 may have bottomed, with some strategic positioning indicating renewed, albeit cautious, interest from institutional allocators.

What Investors Are Watching

Fed outcomes: Interest rate expectations and liquidity guidance from the Fed’s latest meeting remain pivotal for near-term price direction.

Volatility trends: Recent week’s stabilisation points to reduced, not eliminated, volatility with range-bound trading likely until clearer macro cues emerge.

Institutional activity: Fund flows and strategic positioning by large holders and institutional funds will continue shaping market breadth.

Bottom Line: Bitcoin and Ethereum exhibited measured price action amid a cautious market backdrop. With macroeconomic signals and liquidity expectations at the forefront of investor focus, the digital asset sector remains in a strategic consolidation phase, with participants awaiting clarity before committing to new directional positions.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Ends Lower As Key Indices Retreat
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Wednesday, with the FBM KLCI falling 14.76 points, or 0.83%, to 1,756.49 amid broad-based selling across major sectors.

The FBM70 slipped 150.84 points, or 0.85%, to 17,563.23, while the F4GBM eased 9.13 points, or 0.87%, to 1,035.05. The FBMEMAS and FBMSHA indices also retreated, shedding 112.31 points (-0.87%) and 91.94 points (-0.74%) respectively.

Market activity was led by ISF, which surged 0.150 to close at 0.480 with a trading volume of over 264.5 million shares. CapitalA gained 0.010 to 0.585 on volume of 69.2 million, while Velesto slipped 0.010 to 0.305 despite 66 million shares changing hands.

Tanco and Zetrix closed at 1.340 and 0.820 respectively, with minimal net changes, reflecting selective buying in small-cap counters.

The market’s overall retreat was attributed to cautious investor sentiment as global markets weigh geopolitical uncertainties and mixed corporate earnings.

data.title: Healthcare And Plantation Counters Rally As Consumer Stocks Slide
data.description:
On the Bursa Malaysia front, DLADY emerged as the top gainer, rising 0.780 to 33.800 with 661,000 shares traded.

Healthcare giant IHH added 0.310 to 9.020 on heavy volume of 76.2 million, followed by KLK at 20.080 (+0.260) and Allianz at 23.240 (+0.240). CView closed at 3.150, up 0.190, rounding out the top five.

Among the losers, Nestlé led declines, dropping 2.100 to 113.500 with 20.1 million shares exchanged. MPI fell 0.720 to 32.500, while F&N eased 0.500 to 35.500.

KJTS, which has been in the spotlight for its recent partnership developments, retreated 0.310 to 0.820 on volume exceeding 112 million, and CIMB fell 0.300 to 8.650.

The mixed performance reflects investor rotation from defensive consumer stocks to counters with near-term catalysts, as trading volumes remain concentrated in selected heavyweights and high-liquidity small caps.
